# Human Task Manager

**Definition.** Human Task Manager allocates, queues, and tracks human work items generated by processes and cases, providing task lists, assignment rules, escalation, and completion handling.
**Also known as:** Task List Service, User Task Service, Human Workflow Manager.

## Relationships
- Human Task Manager is defined in the Integration & APIs domain.
- Human Task Manager is derived from Workflow Orchestration.
- Human Task Manager depends on BPMN Process Engine.

## Details
Human Task Manager turns BPMN user tasks and CMMN human tasks into actionable work items: it applies assignment rules (role-based, group queues, round-robin), supports claim and delegation, renders task forms, and enforces due dates with timer-based escalation and reassignment. Camunda Tasklist exposes filtered queues so each worker sees only their assignable tasks, and completion writes variables back to the originating process instance. In banking it drives four-eyes approvals, manual KYC review, and exception handling where a person must act before the workflow continues.
